businessModel.text: Revenue from Aeronautics (F-35, F-16, 35%), Missiles & Fire Control (Patriot, HIMARS, 25%), Rotary & Mission Systems (helicopters, 20%), and Space (satellites, classified, 20%). Primarily fixed-price US Government contracts.

recentCatalysts.text: F-35 Block 4+ standard contract awarded Q3 2025 ($8bn lot 17) AUKUS trilateral agreement creates 12+ yr pipeline for nuclear-powered submarine construction Tritorial Battery II Iron Dome award for Middle East allies ($2.2bn) Classified programme revenue grew 18% FY24, now ~35% of total misson
items: F-35 Block 4+ standard contract awarded Q3 2025 ($8bn lot 17)
AUKUS trilateral agreement creates 12+ yr pipeline for nuclear-powered submarine construction
Tritorial Battery II Iron Dome award for Middle East allies ($2.2bn)
Classified programme revenue grew 18% FY24, now ~35% of total misson

keyRisks.text: 1. DoD budget appropriation risk if US Congress raises debt-ceiling conditions tied to cuts 2. Fixed-price F-35 contracts create margin risk on Block 4 complexity overruns 3. Space sector programme delays (e.g. NTS-3 satellite) add fixed-cost drag 4. Labour cost inflation in maritime sector (Newport News shipyard) compresses margins 5. Foreign Military Sales subject to Congressional review — geopolitical freeze risk

risks.risk: 1. DoD budget appropriation risk if US Congress raises debt-ceiling conditions tied to cuts
2. Fixed-price F-35 contracts create margin risk on Block 4 complexity overruns
3. Space sector programme delays (e.g. NTS-3 satellite) add fixed-cost drag
4. Labour cost inflation in maritime sector (Newport News shipyard) compresses margins
5. Foreign Military Sales subject to Congressional review — geopolitical freeze risk

whoShouldOwn.text: Defence-budget beneficiaries seeking exposure to NATO-aligned spending. Geopolitical risk-off positioning. Long duration for capital-light defence primes. NOT suitable for investors expecting high growth or margin expansion beyond low-single digits.

entryExit.text: Establish at $550–$590 (15–20% below 52-week high). HOLD $590–$650. REDUCE above $650. Programme milestone: Sentinel ICBM test launch Q1 FY27. | BUY: Establish at $550–$590 (15–20% below 52-week high). | REDUCE above $650.

sources.marketData: Live quote data sourced via DYOR HQ data pipeline (Google Sheets + Yahoo Finance). Fields include price, market capitalisation, 52-week range, P/E, EPS, and volume.
sources.companyFilings: Public company filings, regulatory announcements, investor presentations, and RNS where referenced in the analysis.
